title: How Nighttime Light Became a Cardiovascular Risk Factor
description: For decades, cardiology treated heart disease as a plumbing problem Ã¢ÂÂ statins for the sludge, stents for the pipes. A landmark 2025 American Heart Association statement now formally names a different culprit: the systematic desynchronization of the body's internal clocks by artificial light, shift work, and the 24/7 economy. The implications reach far beyond sleep hygiene.
